claim: UNCTAD's 2025 maritime review identifies Strait of Hormuz disruption as material to seaborne oil trade, so crude-tanker cycle reads should treat rerouting and trade-flow disruption as transition or exit-risk evidence.
IEA Oil Market Report coverage of refining activity, oil trade, inventories, supply, demand, and prices is directly relevant to product and chemical tanker cycle checks beyond crude-price direction.
UNCTAD reports dry-bulk freight strength in 2024 followed by softening in 2025, making freight-rate direction a contradiction check for any commodity-led dry-bulk recovery signal.
IEA's Q2 2026 gas report highlights LNG supply disruption and strong gas-price volatility, so LNG/LPG shipping reads should check gas balances and route risk before calling continuation.
IEA's May 2026 oil-market deficit and inventory-draw backdrop supports using oil balances and price volatility as offshore vessel and drilling capex-cycle evidence, not as a standalone equity signal.
World Bank's April 2026 commodity outlook frames the energy-price shock as a major macro input, so E&P cycle reads should separate commodity cash-flow support from geopolitical exit risk.
IEA oil-market analysis and EIA Open Data provide public anchors for prices, inventories, supply, and demand before oil-service backlog or order-intake claims are used as cycle evidence.

The Norwegian Seafood Council reported record 2025 seafood export value alongside higher salmon volume, so seafood-cycle reads should check price realization and volume mix rather than headline value alone.

World Bank's April 2026 commodity outlook covers energy, metals, critical minerals, agriculture, precious metals, and fertilizers, making commodity breadth a necessary check for aluminum and metals-cycle recovery claims.
IEA Renewables 2025 emphasizes policy changes, manufacturing trends, and sector financial health, matching the radar's focus on rates, project returns, and supply-chain evidence for renewables.
Norges Bank's 2026 H1 financial-stability report identifies Norwegian banks' substantial commercial-real-estate exposure as a vulnerability, so bank-cycle confidence should reflect CRE credit risk.
source_name: Norges Bank Financial Stability Report 2026 H1

claim: Norges Bank's 2026 H1 financial-stability report says commercial-property selling prices rose at the beginning of 2025 and have since been broadly flat, a transition watch item for real estate.
source_name: Norges Bank Financial Stability Report 2026 H1

claim: IMF's April 2026 World Economic Outlook frames geopolitical tension, energy shocks, inflation pressure, and financial conditions as global-cycle risks, so exporter-cycle reads should require macro confirmation.
